CPU sample dominated by ONNX transformer kernels. Evidence in benchmarks/BASELINES.md and benchmarks/results.json. Focused tests and clippy passed.

title: Watch-mode incremental index: a file save costs <1ms of index work

title: EPIC: Raw-speed dominance: faster than ripgrep, ast-grep, and semgrep at their own game
description: ast-sgrep is complementary to ripgrep/ast-grep/semgrep, but complementary is not an excuse to be slower. The index changes the physics: a pre-built index should beat streaming grep on warm queries, and pre-parsed ASTs should beat re-parsing pattern matchers every time. Endgame: on the committed speed benchmark, ast-sgrep wins lexical vs ripgrep (warm), structural vs ast-grep and semgrep, at every corpus size, with honest cold-start numbers published where we lose.

title: Eval harness: MRR/nDCG/recall@k CLI over benchmarks/gold

## Why
Instrument every ranking bead uses. No later bead may claim numbers this harness did not produce.

## Task
1. Dedicated eval binary/crate over benchmarks/gold
2. Metrics + JSON schema
3. A/B mode with per-query diff
4. Reproduce BASELINES.md numbers exactly
5. Document usage in docs/benchmarks.md

## Acceptance (done_when)
1. Reproduces BASELINES.md numbers exactly
2. JSON schema documented
3. A/B mode works
4. Bench smoke can call harness
5. Independent agent runs one command and gets JSON

## Verify
eval ... --gold gold/self.json matches BASELINES within epsilon.

## Out of scope
Training models; only measurement

## Handoff
CLI --help + sample JSON path.

Example: a query combining a structural function pattern, a semantic clause like "retry with backoff", and a path filter in one string. ACCEPTANCE: spec in docs/QUERY_GRAMMAR.md; parser goldens + error-message quality tests; all three channels reachable from one query string; old syntaxes still work.

title: Hard single-core CPU ceiling for asgrep and builds
description: User-observed asgrep and rustc exceeded 100% CPU during scale work. Enforce a strict default ceiling below one logical core for every asgrep execution path, including neural inference, indexing, search, rerank, benchmarks, and child processes. Repository build/test workflows must invoke rustc through a per-process limiter and CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1. ACCEPTANCE: sustained index/search/eval and a forced clean Rust compile sampled across process trees never exceed 95% aggregate CPU plus a documented <=5% sampler tolerance; focused regression harness fails on violations; performance measurements are produced under the same cap.
close_reason: Added product-wide Unix supervisor and repo rustc wrapper: default/max 80%, 10ms duty cycle, forced single-thread BLAS/Rayon/ONNX, authenticated worker handoff, exact signal/exit propagation, Ctrl-Z safety, descendant cleanup, and fail-closed unsupported platforms. Verified 61 CLI unit + 6 live supervisor + 16 wrapper tests, clippy -D warnings, 100k observed 81.4% and 446.7MiB RSS (never 100%).

title: Embedder trait: isolate hashed embedder behind pluggable backend

Zero behavior change gate: hybrid == current on all corpora with hashed backend selected. ACCEPTANCE: trait landed, goldens green, A/B via eval harness shows zero delta.
close_reason: Embedder trait landed (embed_batch/embed/dim/model_id/cost_hint) with HashedEmbedder/OllamaEmbedder/CloudEmbedder impls and embedder_for() selection; provider.rs routes through the trait with identical fallback semantics. Verified: cargo test -p ast-sgrep-embed (13 tests), cargo test -p ast-sgrep-lang/core/cli (all pass incl. retrieval_gold), clippy --features cloud clean, fmt clean, and zero-delta A/B via frozen git-worktree corpus (MRR/nDCG/recall identical to 3 decimals, MRR=0.712 as expected). Commit 8c510df.

title: Implement deterministic index freshness and concurrency
description: Make indexed results trustworthy without hidden daemons. First search in an unindexed project lazily indexes it. Later searches incrementally refresh changes without duplicate rescans during immediate follow-ups. Concurrent same-root calls cannot race SQLite writers, while independent roots remain parallel.
design: Use one-shot subprocesses, per-root freshness state, and a promise or mutex. Refresh when index is missing, root is dirty, or interval expires. Known Pi write and edit results can mark dirty, while timed refresh catches shell, user, and external edits. Do not start asgrep watch by default.
acceptance_criteria: E2E tests cover first-use indexing, repeat-search deduplication, create/modify/delete visibility, external edits after interval, known Pi write and edit tool results marking the root dirty for the very next search, same-root serialization, cross-root parallelism, failed-index retry, cancellation cleanup, and no orphan processes. Missing, incompatible, or known-dirty indexes are never silently used.

Feeds wlp-embedder-quality-8370 (real local embeddings) ŌĆö this corpus becomes its gate. Also positions ast-sgrep as the drop-in recall backend for any capn-style chart layer (see fszero-afo).

title: LSP: failed background index leaves workspace/symbol permanently empty
description:
## Kind

title: Red-test: lazy IVF embed path vs ANN L2-normalized ranking divergence
description:

`embed_pass_lazy_ivf` ranks candidates via `rank_chunk_indices_by_vector` (raw store vectors + plain dot) while ANN/full path uses `flatten_vectors_for_search` L2-normalize then cos/dot. On non-unit-norm embeddings, top-k order diverges.

title: Duty cycle CONT is wall-time not multi-core CPU ceiling

## Kind

title: LSP index_lock Mutex::lock has no timeout (request wait unbounded)
description:
## Kind

Product has no stated max-wait for LSP requests blocked on index_lock while background index_all holds the mutex. Without fair release modeling, bounded-wait liveness FAILS.

title: ERROR_BUDGET: cold index burn_rate=20 under p95 SLO (error_rate=1.0)
description:

title: ERROR_BUDGET: variance envelope (Ōēż10% drift) conflicts with hard thr (100% over)
description:
## Finding

title: SLO recovery: ~4.6% embed or ~3.0% SQLite speedup closes cold index mean gap
description:
## Finding

SemanticLocalEmbedding share 41.1% ŌåÆ embed_msŌēł104.7 ŌåÆ required embed-only speedup **Ōēł4.58%**.
upsert_file share 62.6% ŌåÆ sqlite-only speedup **Ōēł3.01%**.
Either small win closes *mean* gap; p95 still 258ms so need slightly more for p95 thr.
Aligns with BUDGETS optimization ranks (prepared stmt, skip embed, batch upsert) with quantified break-even.

## Evidence

- hotspot_table.md ranks 1ŌĆō2
- semantic embed path: store/sqlite embed_chunks; embed SemanticLocalEmbedding

## Acceptance
When prioritizing index budget recovery, treat break-even fractions as gate: land Ōēź5% embed or Ōēź3% SQLite wall reduction on same host and re-run error_budget_cold_index.

title: Measure hybrid search T1/T_inf (work-span/Brent) before N-core speedup claims

title: FP gate: MIN_SIMILARITY membership not scale-invariant under query scale (product-dot)
description:
## Kind
counterexample

## Claim
Under product-dot `cosine_similarity`, exclusive `sim > MIN_SIMILARITY` (0.08) membership for a fixed corpus is **not** invariant when the query is positively scaled. True cosine membership is stable under the same transform.

## Evidence

- base query [0.1,0,0,0]: pass indices {1,2}; query├Ś2: pass {0,1,2} (row0 0.05ŌåÆ0.10)
- Distinct from `5du.16` (single-score scale invariance) and `5du.15` (exact equality drop): this is **set-membership** under scale
- Related: `jk7.2`, `5du.16`

## Acceptance
- [ ] Gate uses true cosine OR both sides L2-normalized before thr OR document magnitude coupling
- [ ] Re-run probe: product membership stable under query scale OR thr applied post-normalize

## Labels
counterexample, math-review, pass-16, numerical-fp

title: FP: top_k_flat / top_by admit +inf (missing is_finite vs top_k_similarity)
description:
## Kind
counterexample

## Claim
`top_k_similarity` filters `sim.is_finite()` before the exclusive min gate (`math.rs:59`). `top_k_flat_similarity` (`math.rs:78-79`) and `top_by_similarity` (`math.rs:119`) only check `sim > min`, so **+inf** ranks first. NaNs are dropped by comparison falsehood / sort policy, but Inf survives.

## Evidence
- `passes/pass-16/evidence/fp_cosine_contract_probes.json` CE_FP_INF_FLAT + CE_FP_F32_OVERFLOW
- Mirror: flat admits (0, inf); finite_guard excludes inf
- f32 overflow of large unnormalized components ŌåÆ inf feeds this path
- Hot call sites: ANN flat scan, `rank_chunk_indices_by_vector` ŌåÆ top_by

## Acceptance

- [ ] Unit test: scored list with inf/nan does not return inf hits

title: FP: MIN_SIMILARITY exclusive gate ULP-unstable near binary boundary
description:
## Kind
counterexample

## Claim
Intended products near `MIN_SIMILARITY=0.08` are not exact in binary f32/f64. Example: query 0.1 ┬Ę row 0.8 yields ~0.08000000000000002 which **passes** exclusive `>`, while exact 0.08 would fail (`5du.15`). Gate decisions at the threshold are ULP-sensitive.

## Evidence
- `passes/pass-16/evidence/fp_cosine_contract_probes.json` CE_FP_THRESH_SCALE product_pass_base includes (2, 0.08000000000000002)
- Complements exclusive-equality CE `5du.15` with a floating-point boundary witness

## Acceptance
- [ ] Document FP threshold policy OR use epsilon band OR true cosine in [-1,1]
- [ ] Red test near 0.08 with binary-inexact factors

## Labels
counterexample, math-review, pass-16, numerical-fp

title: Clock: Pi freshness/timeout 30s lease fails skew budget; backward jump can serve stale
description:
## Finding (pass-15 T-tier clock)
packages/pi FreshnessCoordinator and AstSgrepRuntime use wall-clock DEFAULT_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS=DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000.
